hook_check.expect: prose describing reverse-direction check that iterates diff_files against dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created and raises files_modified_underreport_violation with severity critical for any absent path not in baseline_dirty_paths

baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, and dev.observed_preexisting present in both the aggregate construction rule text (lines ~720-734) and the example aggregate JSON (lines ~739-756); BOTH baseline_head_sha mismatch AND baseline_dirty_snapshot mismatch described as blocking (not warning); a dev implementation that blocks SHA mismatch only while ignoring dirty-snapshot mismatch does not satisfy this criterion
agents/dev.md line ~495 derivation rule references both git ls-files --others --exclude-standard AND git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A as the combined files_created derivation
agents/qa.md line ~427 QA files_created check references git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A as part of the combined derivation set it compares against
reverse check exclusion set is baseline_dirty_paths (parsed from baseline_dirty_snapshot porcelain); observed_preexisting is not listed as an exclusion in the reverse check logic

reverse check compares diff_files against dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created (union), not against dev.files_modified alone; any path in dev.files_created satisfies the union check and does not raise the violation
